scope: Algebra I foundation lesson content correctness
findings: All three methods - graphing, substitution, elimination - are mathematically correct and reach the same kind of ordered-pair solution.
Worked example (3, 7) by substitution and (3, 2) by elimination both verify when checked back into the original equations.
Pause-and-try problems (2, 4) and (4, 0) are correct and consistent with the narrated solutions on the answer-reveal slides.
The one / none / infinite case panel correctly maps slope-intercept relationships to false / always-true algebra outcomes.
The trap slide correctly emphasizes that every term must scale during elimination, not just the matching coefficient.
release_notes: Foundation math only; no AP, College Board, Common App, CEEB, accreditation, NCAA, or college-admissions claims.
